ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sightings" (
  "sighting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sighting */,
  "sighting_date" TEXT /* Date of the sighting */,
  "species_id" INTEGER /* ID of the species sighted */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the sighting location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the sighting location */,
  "number" INTEGER /* Number of individuals sighted */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the sighting */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the sighting */,
  "time_of_day" TEXT /* Time of day of the sighting */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the sighting */,
  "observer_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments from the observer */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the sighting data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sighting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sightings_latitude FOREIGN KEY ("latitude") REFERENCES locations ("latitude"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sightings_longitude FOREIGN KEY ("longitude") REFERENCES locations ("longitude"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sightings_species_id FOREIGN KEY ("species_id") REFERENCES species ("species_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "species" (
  "species_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each species */,
  "species_name" TEXT /* Name of the species */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the species (e.g., mammal, bird, reptile) */,
  "common_name" TEXT /* Common name of the species */,
  "scientific_name" TEXT /* Scientific name of the species */,
  "conservation_status" TEXT /* Conservation status of the species */,
  "taxonomic_class" TEXT /* Taxonomic class of the species */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("species_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., park, reserve, city) */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location */,
  "habitat" TEXT /* Habitat type of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "migration_patterns" (
  "pattern_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each migration pattern */,
  "species_id" INTEGER /* ID of the species */,
  "start_location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the starting location */,
  "end_location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ending location */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the migration */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the migration */,
  "migration_type" TEXT /* Type of migration */,
  "distance" REAL /* Distance of the migration */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the migration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pattern_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_migration_patterns_species_id FOREIGN KEY ("species_id") REFERENCES species ("species_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_migration_patterns_start_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("start_location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_migration_patterns_end_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("end_location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, conservationist, admin) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization or affiliation of the user */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sighting_contributors" (
  "contributor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contributor */,
  "sighting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sighting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who contributed */,
  "contribution_type" TEXT /* Type of contribution (e.g., photographer, spotter, validator) */,
  "contribution_date" TEXT /* Date of the contribution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contributor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sighting_contributors_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sighting_contributors_sighting_id FOREIGN KEY ("sighting_id") REFERENCES sightings ("sighting_id")
)
column_names: Time of day of the sighting
Additional notes or comments from the observer
Category of the species (e.g., mammal, bird, reptile)
Name of the location (e.g., park, reserve, city)
Unique identifier for each migration pattern
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, conservationist, admin)
Organization or affiliation of the user
ID of the user who contributed
Type of contribution (e.g., photographer, spotter, validator)
